configuration.aws.credentials.descriptio:
Variables used to configure AWS credentials.

Example: to set the AWS region and profile, add items with keys `AWS_REGION` and `AWS_PROFILE`.
configuration.aws.inferenceProfileRegion:
Override the inference profile region for AWS services.

By default, the inference profile region is derived from `AWS_REGION` (e.g., 'us-east-1' -> 'us'). Use this setting to explicitly specify a different region.

Examples: 'global', 'us', 'eu', 'apac'.
configuration.snowflake.credentials.desc:
Variables used to configure Snowflake Cortex provider.

Example: to set the Snowflake account and connections path, add items with keys `SNOWFLAKE_ACCOUNT` and `SNOWFLAKE_HOME`.
configuration.snowflake.customHeaders.de: Extra HTTP headers attached to every Snowflake Cortex request. **Do not set headers managed by the underlying SDKs** (`Authorization` derived from your bearer token, `x-api-key`, `anthropic-version`, `Content-Type`) — on Claude chat requests a colliding entry will override the SDK's auth/version header and break the connection. Non-Claude routes through a fetch wrapper that enforces additive-only precedence.
configuration.anthropic.baseUrl.descript: Custom Anthropic API base URL. Overrides the default https://api.anthropic.com/v1 endpoint. Also set automatically from the ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL environment variable.
configuration.anthropic.customHeaders.de: Extra HTTP headers attached to every Anthropic request. Useful for enterprise gateways that require tenancy or routing headers. **Do not set headers managed by the Anthropic SDK** (`x-api-key`, `anthropic-version`, `Content-Type`) — on chat requests a colliding entry will override the SDK's auth/version header and break the connection.
configuration.foundry.customHeaders.desc: Extra HTTP headers attached to every Microsoft Foundry request. Additive only — entries that collide with headers set by the underlying OpenAI SDK (`Authorization` derived from your API key, `Content-Type`) are ignored at request time.
configuration.openai-api.baseUrl.descrip: Custom OpenAI API base URL. Overrides the default https://api.openai.com/v1 endpoint. Also set automatically from the OPENAI_BASE_URL environment variable.
configuration.openai-api.customHeaders.d: Extra HTTP headers attached to every OpenAI request. Useful for enterprise gateways that require tenancy or routing headers. **Do not set headers managed by the OpenAI SDK** (`Authorization`, `Content-Type`) — on chat requests a colliding entry will override the SDK's auth header and break the connection.
configuration.google.baseUrl.description: Custom Gemini API base URL. Overrides the default https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta endpoint. Also set automatically from the GEMINI_BASE_URL environment variable.
configuration.google.customHeaders.descr: Extra HTTP headers attached to every Gemini request. **Do not set headers managed by the Google Generative AI SDK** (`x-goog-api-key`, `Content-Type`) — on chat requests a colliding entry will override the SDK's headers and break the connection.
Base URL for the custom OpenAI-compatible provider endpoint.
configuration.openai-compatible.customHe: Extra HTTP headers attached to every OpenAI-compatible request (e.g., Databricks gateway requirements like `x-databricks-use-coding-agent-mode`). Additive only — entries that collide with headers set by the OpenAI SDK (`Authorization` derived from your API key, `Content-Type`) are ignored at request time.
